content: When extracting loop-inlined blocks from scheduler.sh's poll loop into named stage_* functions, prefer strict 1:1 extraction over consolidating similar-looking blocks (e.g. two non-dispatching gate stages). Blocks that look interchangeable often each own a distinct cross-stage side-channel variable (e.g. CI_BLOCKED vs RESCUED) consumed by a later stage — merging them muddies which stage owns which output for no structural gain. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-07-scheduler-internal-seams-design.md (#185).

scheduler.sh's SCHEDULER_SOURCE_ONLY=1 sourcing tests (test_scheduler.sh, test_dispatch_ceiling.sh, test_config_deletion.sh, test_159_regression.sh, test_has_new_comment_after_report.sh, test_scheduler_pagination.sh) must keep sourcing scheduler.sh itself, not any extracted sourceable predicate lib (scripts/scheduler_lib.sh) — they exercise functions that intentionally stay in scheduler.sh (dispatch, is_issue_running, fetch_board_items, trip_to_blocked) alongside lib-only pure predicates, because those functions call gh/docker/dispatch/set_board_status side effects. New lib-only unit coverage belongs in a separate dedicated test file that sources the lib directly, not retrofitted into the six existing files. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-07-scheduler-internal-seams-design.md (#185).
